title: Two Nights on Lemmon
summary:
Since 2017, I have had a personal climbing goal to climb at least one 5.12 every year. When I started getting into running a few years ago, I was still climbing enough that this goal was difficult, but obtainable. However, this year I have ran more and climbed less than at any point in the last ten years, meaning I hadn't yet done a 5.12 in 2025. My mini-goal of this outing was to check off the climbing goal. After doing a few warm up climbs and then trying a 5.12, it quickly became very apparent that it was not going to happen. While I felt a little sad in the moment, in the days and week since, it has felt refreshing and that I wasn't able to accomplish it. When we have been climbing since that day, it has just been because it is an activity I love, and not because I'm chasing an arbitrary goal. So, while the climbing objective was a fail for our trip to Mt Lemmon, it was still a great trip. We had our first campfire of van life, ate s'mores, enjoyed two beautiful sunsets, and Maya got to enjoy a nice hike along the Arizona Trail.

-Colin

photos.caption: A view from our camp the first night on Mt Lemmon.
